title: salix × fragilis var. russelliana
intro: Salix × fragilis var. russelliana, also known as the brittle willow or Russell's willow, is a plant hybrid derived from the weeping willow (Salix babylonica) and the crack willow (Salix fragilis). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that may help soothe wounds, reduce pain and inflammation, and combat infections. The health benefits of Salix × fragilis var. russelliana include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce symptoms of anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally to treat wounds and skin conditions, or internally to support overall health and well-being.
